summary: Security option for whether the last signed-in username is shown at sign-in. Local Windows security metadata documents the same Policies\System\DontDisplayLastUserName value that the app writes.

setting.name: Display of the Last Signed-In Username
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can show the name of the last person who signed in on the sign-in screen. This setting decides whether that username is shown or hidden.
setting.why_people_change_it: People hide the last signed-in username on shared or public-facing systems so the sign-in screen reveals less information about who uses the device.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding it improves privacy at the sign-in screen, but it also removes a convenience hint that makes it easier for the usual user to see which account signed in last.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents this as the security option 'Interactive logon: Do not display last user name'. The local Windows security INF files map it to HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DontDisplayLastUserName with a default of 0. The app uses the conventional enabled-state value of 1 to hide the username.

allowed_values.label: Show the last signed-in username
allowed_values.meaning: The security option is not enabled, so the last signed-in username can appear at sign-in.

allowed_values.label: Hide the last signed-in username
allowed_values.meaning: The security option is enabled and the last signed-in username is not shown on the sign-in screen.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DontDisplayLastUserName = 1, which matches the documented enabled security-option behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows security-option default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows client systems exposing the interactive logon security option

states.rationale: The local Windows security baseline files list a default of 0.

windows_defaults.notes: By default, Windows does not force the username to be hidden through this security option.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default

states.rationale: Keep the normal sign-in cue visible.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the sign-in screen to show which account was used last.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You do not want account names exposed on the sign-in screen.
tradeoffs: Sign-in is slightly more convenient for the usual user.
The sign-in screen exposes more identity information.

recommended_profiles.label: Private sign-in screen
avoid_for: Users who want the most convenient sign-in flow

states.rationale: Hide the last signed-in username at the sign-in screen.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the sign-in screen to reveal less information about who uses the device.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the usual user to see the last account name immediately.
tradeoffs: Users must remember or type their username instead of relying on the visible last account.

evidence.title: Microsoft security policy setting: Interactive logon: Do not display last user name
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the purpose of the security option, its enabled and disabled behavior, and its default state.

evidence.title: Local Windows security option registry mapping
evidence.summary: The local Windows security registry metadata maps the security option to HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DontDisplayLastUserName.

evidence.title: Local Windows default security baseline entry
evidence.summary: The local Windows default security baseline lists DontDisplayLastUserName with default value 0.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DontDisplayLastUserName = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the security-option behavior for hiding the last signed-in username, and local Windows security metadata explicitly maps that option to Policies\System\DontDisplayLastUserName with a default of 0. The app writes the expected enabled-state value of 1.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Windows security metadata maps the security option directly to Policies/System/DontDisplayLastUserName. evidence/files/external/c/Windows/inf/defltbase.inf.md also confirms the default value 0, and Microsoft documents the enabled behavior as hiding the last signed-in username.
